meaning: adj.不可靠的,靠不住的 不可信赖的
examples: I will not swap my money for your undependable love.
But Internet service in Uganda proved slow and undependable.
It could be that your partners are being flaky or you could be the undependable one.
And they arranged a trade for the best center in the game Dwight Howard and shipped off moody and undependable Andrew Bynum.
Traditional conservatives view the federal government as being untrustworthy and undependable.
